description: A corpus of REAL natural-language prompts that have actually been used against Isolinear during development (harvested from BDD evidence, evals, tests, STATUS/HANDOFF, and git history). Intended as a shared, stable set for testing and model benchmarking — planner quality, entity selection, render-family routing, and (ADR-0029) codegen accept/reject/repair rates.
provenance: Assembled 2026-07-01 by trawling the working tree + full git history of the isolinear repo. Each prompt below appeared verbatim (or near-verbatim) in a captured artifact; `expect` is the intended/observed system behavior, not a guarantee.
how_to_use: Load `prompts`. For planner/selection benchmarking, feed `prompt` and score against `expect.render_family` / `expect.behavior`. For ADR-0029 codegen benchmarking, run the resolved ChartSpec+data through generate_chart_code -> worker sandbox and tally accept/reject/repair per model. `category` groups by capability; `edge` flags non-happy-path cases.

render_families.time_series_overlay: one numeric primary + one/more binary entities shaded as intervals behind it (ADR-0022 D4/D5)
render_families.timeline: binary/categorical step track(s), no numeric axis (ADR-0022)
render_families.histogram: distribution of a single numeric entity (ADR-0023)
render_families.clarification: genuinely ambiguous -> the planner asks, never guesses (invariant #1)
render_families.refuse: out of scope for the read-only MVP (e.g. mutation) -> decline / clarify (invariant #2)

prompts.prompt: Show the upstairs temperature
Show upstairs temperature for the last day
Show the bathroom temperature
Show the family room temperature
Show office temperature
show kitchen temperature today
show humidity
Show me the temperature over time
show upstairs temperature overnight
attic temperature last weekend
Show me the attic temperature over the last 46 hours
show me the kitchen door state over the last four hours
show me the distribution of upstairs temperature this week
Compare upstairs and downstairs temperatures
Compare upstairs and downstairs temperatures over the last 24 hours
Show the family room temperature and when the AC was running
show kitchen temp and when the AC was running
prompts.notes: Historically triggered entity-selection over-composition ('kitchen' noise-matched the ecobee temp sensor); fixed by ADR-0028 model-validated composition prune.

prompts.prompt: show me the temperature and when the AC was running
show me maren's room temperature and when the AC was running
prompts.notes: 'AC' has no token overlap with the ecobee entity name; historically needed D2 expansion / semantic alias (ADR-0024).

prompts.prompt: show me the kitchen temperature yesterday and when the kitchen door was open
kitchen temperature and the front door
when was the kitchen door open today
prompts.notes: Historically failed via over-composition with a temp sensor; ADR-0028.

prompts.prompt: show me when the kitchen door was open this morning
Mark when the dishwasher was running over the last day
when was the air conditioning on
show me the air conditioning
prompts.notes: Terse; a climate/binary entity, no explicit metric.

prompts.prompt: show the distribution of bathroom temp
show distribution of upstairs temp
what's the average temperature per day
what's the average upstairs temperature per day
family room average temperature per day
Show sensor.upstairs_temperature for the last 24 hours
Show sensor.family_room_sensor_temperature
Show binary_sensor.kitchen_door for the last 24 hours
Show sensor.upstairs_temperature binary_sensor.kitchen_door for the last 24 hours
upstairs temperature
prompts.notes: Three upstairs temperature sensors -> planner asks 'Should I average them and call that upstairs temperature?' (invariant #1).

prompts.prompt: Show the temperature
prompts.notes: Which temperature entity? Depends on the configured allowlist.

prompts.prompt: show thermostat history
Use and remember as upstairs temperature

expect.behavior: save SemanticAlias on clarification answer (ADR-0009, Tranche 2)

prompts.notes: Follow-up to an entity clarification; a reworded prompt should then skip clarification.

prompts.prompt: Turn on the kitchen lights
prompts.notes: Mutation request; the read-only MVP must not act (invariant #2).

prompts.prompt: Show light.kitchen compared with the approved climate sensor
prompts.notes: Mixes a non-charted/likely-unapproved entity with an approved one; allowlist boundary applies (invariant #1).

prompts.prompt: Render unsupported energy histogram

expect.behavior: fail closed with a clear unsupported result, not a bad render
